user_query:
I’m helping the arcade prep a prototype promo pack for our upcoming Retro Reel & High Score Night. Please use the provided local inputs to generate clean promo copy, a quick engagement summary from past events, and a concise one-page event brief, then validate the outputs.

Inputs (read-only):
- input/promos_draft.txt (draft blurbs)
- input/events.csv (past event metrics)
- input/lineup.json (upcoming event lineup)

Deliverables (write these files):
1) out/promos_rewritten.txt
   - Rewrite each line from input/promos_draft.txt to be upbeat, clearer, and under 140 characters.
   - Keep the original line order; produce exactly one rewritten line per input line.
   - Each rewritten line must include either the exact event name "Retro Reel & High Score Night" (from input/lineup.json) or the hashtag "#ArcadeNight".
   - Use at most one exclamation mark (!) per line.

2) out/engagement_summary.csv
   - Compute basic aggregates from input/events.csv grouped by event_type.
   - Columns: event_type, events_count, total_tickets, total_tokens, avg_survey_rating.
   - events_count = number of rows per group; totals are integer sums; avg_survey_rating is the mean per group rounded to 2 decimals.

3) out/event_brief.md
   - Include the event name and date from input/lineup.json.
   - Add a Lineup section listing the featured_movie and arcade_games from input/lineup.json.
   - Add a Benchmarks section summarizing the row for "Retro Reel & High Score Night" from out/engagement_summary.csv (show the numeric values you computed for that event type). If that event type is absent, include overall averages instead.
   - Add a Suggested copy section with the first three lines from out/promos_rewritten.txt as bullet points.

4) tests/validate.py and out/validation.log
   - Create a simple validation script at tests/validate.py that verifies at minimum:
     • out/promos_rewritten.txt has the same number of lines as input/promos_draft.txt.
     • Every rewritten line is <= 140 characters, contains either "Retro Reel & High Score Night" or "#ArcadeNight", and has <= 1 exclamation mark.
     • out/engagement_summary.csv contains a header with the required columns and at least one row per distinct event_type in input/events.csv.
     • out/event_brief.md includes the event name from input/lineup.json and the Benchmarks and Suggested copy sections.
   - Run the validator and save its stdout to out/validation.log. The script should exit non-zero if any check fails.

Notes:
- Keep everything local—no external services. Use the inputs as the single source of truth.
- Make the rewritten promos crisp and friendly but don’t invent facts not present in the inputs.
- Paths must match exactly as specified above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
